channel.title: Ghosxt Blog — Managed IT & Cybersecurity for the Central Coast
channel.description: Practical IT, cybersecurity, and compliance guidance for small businesses on California's Central Coast, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: USB Drop Attacks: How a "Lost" Flash Drive Becomes a Ransomware Foothold (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A USB drive left in a parking lot or mailed to your office can hand an attacker a foothold before anyone clicks a link. How USB drop attacks work and how to stop them.

item.title: Malvertising: How Fake Software Ads Are Infecting Small Businesses (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Malicious search ads impersonate PuTTY, Zoom, and other downloads, infecting employees before they reach the real site. How malvertising works and how to stop it.

item.title: ClickFix: The Fake CAPTCHA Attack Tricking Employees Into Infecting Their Own PC (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: ClickFix pages pose as a CAPTCHA or a Zoom error and talk employees into pasting a command into Windows Run. How the attack bypasses antivirus and how to stop it.

item.title: Evil Twin Wi-Fi: How Fake Hotspots Steal Data From Remote Workers (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Evil twin Wi-Fi hotspots copy a real network's name to intercept logins and files from remote and traveling employees. How the attack works and how to stop it.

item.title: Session Hijacking: How Stolen Cookies Let Attackers Skip Past MFA (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Session hijacking steals the cookie that proves you're already logged in, so attackers walk past MFA without a password or a code. How it works and how to stop it.

item.title: Typosquatting: How Lookalike Domains Impersonate Your Business (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Typosquatting uses lookalike domains to impersonate your business or your vendors for phishing and invoice fraud. How it works and how to stop it in 2026.

item.title: SIM Swapping: How Attackers Hijack Your Phone Number to Get Into Business Accounts (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: SIM swapping hijacks your phone number to intercept SMS codes and reset passwords. How it works, the warning signs, and the fixes that stop it in 2026.

item.title: Credential Stuffing: How Reused Passwords Let Attackers Into Your Business Accounts (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Credential stuffing doesn't hack your business — it logs in with a password your employee reused somewhere else. How the attack works, the warning signs, and the fixes that actually stop it in 2026.

item.title: Mobile Device Management (MDM) for Small Business: When You Actually Need It (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: BYOD policies cover personal phones, but company-owned devices, regulated data, and field teams often outgrow that. What MDM actually does, the signs you need more than conditional access, and how to roll it out without over-buying.

item.title: Passkeys for Small Business: Should You Ditch Passwords in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Passkeys now work across Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and most banking apps, and they can't be phished. What a passkey actually is, where it beats passwords and MFA, and a realistic rollout plan for a small business.

item.title: LegacyHive: Nightmare-Eclipse's New Windows Zero-Day, Explained
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: LegacyHive: Nightmare-Eclipse's new Windows privilege-escalation zero-day, unpatched with no CVE yet. What it does and what to do this week.

item.title: Vishing: How Voice Phishing Calls Are Scamming Small Businesses in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A phone call claiming to be your bank, the IRS, or your own IT department is one of the oldest scams in the book, and it still works. How vishing calls are targeting small businesses in 2026, why caller ID can't be trusted, and the verification habits that stop it.

item.title: July 2026 Patch Tuesday: A Record 570 Fixes, 3 Zero-Days, and What an SMB Should Patch First
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: July 2026 Patch Tuesday: a record 570 fixes and 3 zero-days, including AD FS and SharePoint flaws. What a small business should patch first.

item.title: AI Agents at Work: The Non-Human Identity Risk Small Businesses Aren't Managing
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: AI agents can now send email, book meetings, and update your CRM on their own, using real credentials plugged into your systems. Why these non-human identities are a growing small-business risk, and the checklist that governs them.

item.title: Zero Trust Security for Small Business: What It Actually Means in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Zero Trust gets thrown around as a marketing buzzword, but the actual model is simple: never trust, always verify. What it really means for a 10-person business, what it costs, and where to start.

item.title: ADA Website Accessibility for Small Business: What California Law Requires (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Website accessibility demand letters are hitting small businesses across California, and the Unruh Act makes it more expensive here than almost anywhere else. What ADA and WCAG 2.1 AA actually require, why size doesn't exempt you, and the fixes that close most of the risk.

item.title: Smishing: How Text Message Phishing Is Hitting Small Businesses in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Fake delivery texts, fake toll bills, fake payroll alerts — smishing (SMS phishing) skips your spam filter and lands directly on employee phones. How it works, why it's outrunning email phishing, and the small-business controls that stop it.

item.title: How Much Does a Small Business Website Cost in 2026? Real Numbers
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Real 2026 website pricing for small businesses: what a professional 5-page site, a full business site, and an e-commerce build actually cost, what drives the price up, why the $500 site and the $15,000 site are both usually the wrong buy, and what ongoing maintenance should run.

item.title: 9 Signs Your Small Business Website Is Costing You Customers (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A slow, broken, or outdated website sends customers to competitors. The 9 warning signs it's time to fix or rebuild, and which are security risks.

item.title: OAuth App Phishing: How Attackers Get Into Microsoft 365 Without Stealing a Password (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Attackers don't need your password or your MFA code anymore — they just need you to click 'Accept' on a fake app permission request. How OAuth consent phishing works, why it slips past MFA entirely, and the small-business controls that stop it.

item.title: California Data Breach Notification Law: What Small Businesses Must Do After a Breach (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: California's data breach notification law applies to your business no matter how small it is. What actually counts as a reportable breach, what a compliant notice must say, and why the real financial risk is a CPRA lawsuit, not a fine.

item.title: BYOD for Small Business: Securing Personal Phones and Laptops Without Locking Down Everyone's Devices (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Your team is already checking company email on personal phones, whether or not you have a policy for it. What a BYOD policy actually needs to cover, and how to secure personal devices without an enterprise MDM budget.

item.title: The Seasonal Worker IT Checklist: Onboarding and Offboarding Summer Staff Without Leaving Accounts Open
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Harvest crews, tasting-room staff, and summer hospitality hires all need system access, and few businesses ever turn it back off. A simple onboarding and offboarding checklist for seasonal workers on the Central Coast, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Vendor & Third-Party Risk: What Happens When a Vendor You Trust Gets Breached (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A breach at your payroll processor, CRM, or help desk vendor can expose your data even if your own network is never touched. How vendor risk actually works, and the checklist that manages it without a compliance team.

item.title: Domain Name Security: How Small Businesses Lose Their Website and Email Without Getting Hacked (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: An expired renewal, a stolen registrar login, or one unauthorized DNS change can take down your website and email overnight, with no malware involved. How small businesses lose their domain, and the checklist that locks it down in 2026.

item.title: Summer Travel & Remote Work Security: Protecting Company Data When Your Team Isn't in the Office (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Employees working from airports, hotels, and vacation rentals this summer expand your attack surface past the office network you control. The public Wi-Fi, lost-device, and travel-phishing risks that actually cause breaches, and the small business checklist that closes them.

item.title: EDR vs. Antivirus: Why Small Businesses Need More Than a Signature Scanner in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Traditional antivirus catches malware it already recognizes. It misses fileless attacks and the human-operated ransomware crews actually hitting small businesses in 2026. What EDR adds, what it costs, and the 10-minute check to see what you already have.

item.title: Employee Offboarding IT Checklist: Closing the Security Gaps When Someone Leaves
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Former employees keep access to email, cloud files, and business apps far longer than owners realize. The same-day IT offboarding checklist that closes the gap: accounts, devices, shared logins, and forwarding rules.

item.title: Dark Web Monitoring for Small Business: What It Finds, What It Misses, and Whether It's Worth It in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Dark web monitoring scans criminal marketplaces for your employees' stolen credentials. Here is what it actually catches, what it misses, and when it pays off for a small business.

item.title: Shadow IT: The Unauthorized Apps Creating Security Gaps in Your Small Business
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: What shadow IT actually is, why AI tools and SaaS apps make it worse in 2026, and the three-step process for bringing unauthorized software under control before it becomes a breach entry point.
